name: Priority Inversion
summary: A trap that tempts the player to let size, noise, or confidence override actual order.
tell: The loudest or biggest mover looks important but is not automatically first.
countermeasure: Restate the rule and compare arrival, position, and signal before moving.

name: False Yield
summary: A trap that makes the player wait when the rule already allows them to proceed.
tell: The crossing is clear but hesitation keeps the vehicle frozen.
countermeasure: Check the rule, confirm the gap, then go.

name: Stop-Sign Bait
summary: A trap that turns a complete stop into a rolling guess.
tell: The driver slows but never fully stops before moving on.
countermeasure: Make the full stop explicit and then re-scan the crossing.

name: Signal Without Check
summary: A trap that treats the signal as enough without confirming the lane is clear.
tell: The turn indicator is on but mirrors and blind spot were skipped.
countermeasure: Signal, then check mirrors and blind spot before moving.

name: Mirror Lure
summary: A trap that hides an approaching vehicle beside or behind you.
tell: The empty mirror look is misleading because the blind spot still matters.
countermeasure: Use the mirror check as a cue, not a conclusion.

name: Roundabout Confusion
summary: A trap that makes the player enter the circle before the gap is safe.
tell: The circular flow feels urgent even when no clean opening exists.
countermeasure: Wait for the gap and join only when flow is safe.

name: Lane Shear
summary: A trap that pushes the player across a lane boundary without stability.
tell: The move starts before lane position is settled.
countermeasure: Hold lane position, scan, and move only when the lane is truly clear.

name: Pedestrian Ghost
summary: A trap that hides a walker or cyclist behind another vehicle or object.
tell: The view looks open until a body steps into the crossing.
countermeasure: Slow early and expect a hidden pedestrian or cyclist.

name: Late Brake
summary: A trap that asks the player to keep speed too long when the road is narrowing or visibility drops.
tell: The vehicle is committed before the driver has enough sight distance.
countermeasure: Reduce speed before the hazard, not after it.

name: Gap Hunger
summary: A trap that makes the player seize a merge opening that is not actually safe.
tell: The merge feels available because it is available to someone reckless.
countermeasure: Match speed, measure the gap, and wait for a real opening.
